title: westringia capitonia
intro: Westringia capitonia, also known as the coastal rosemary, is a type of flowering plant native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Westringia capitonia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
